name: Bennie Moten
abstract: Benjamin Moten (November 13, 1893 – April 2, 1935) was an American jazz pianist and band leader born and raised in Kansas City, Missouri, United States. He led his Kansas City Orchestra, the most important of the regional, blues-based orchestras active in the Midwest in the 1920s, and helped to develop the riffing style that would come t…

about_quotes.speaker: Ira Gitler
about_quotes.evidence: And I think it was just like in many other situations where jazz flourished and grew... there were a lot of venues for musicians to play. And it was like a happening scene. And so I think when the Basie band started, it came out of other bands as we know, Walter Page’s Blue Devils and Bennie Moten’s
about_quotes.doc_title: Ira Gitler interviewed by Michael Woods, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Schaap
about_quotes.evidence: I knew it was Count Basie with Bennie Moten’s band... I said, 'Well that’s Count Basie playing a little combination Fats Waller and four hands in his early records with Benny Moten’s Kansas City Orchestra'
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Schaap interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: He told me about Charlie Christian... It was somebody else’s band then – Bennie Moten’s band. He paid for them to come to New York. Bennie Moten died.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wendy Oxenhorn
about_quotes.evidence: I would get on the swings and I would hear Bennie Moten, and the Kansas City Five... I remember thinking that was my music.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendy Oxenhorn — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Barefield
about_quotes.evidence: But you were telling me before about the time you were about to pull out, the bus was going to Terre Haute from where? Where were you, with Moten's band? [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah. We had played the dance in Indianapolis that night...

about_quotes.speaker: Count Basie
about_quotes.evidence: "Then joined Bennie Moten's band." "I left Benny's band, then we went to Little Rock."
